name: Cold Open Mid-Action
trigger: drops brain mid-tension, forces question-answering instinct
template: [CHARACTER] was already [doing intense action] when [thing went wrong].
why_it_works: Starting inside the action makes the brain crave the missing context, so it stays to fill the gap.
examples: My hands were shaking before I even opened the email.
I was already on the highway when I realized the brakes were gone.
She hung up before I could say the one thing that mattered.
The fire alarm went off and I was the only one who knew why.
I'd been awake for 40 hours when the offer finally came through.

applied_examples.hook: I was three reps from passing out when the coach said the thing that changed everything.

name: Dead Body Opening
trigger: ending revealed first, creates backward-curiosity loop
template: [Dramatic outcome] had already happened before [we understood the stakes].
why_it_works: Revealing the climax up front replaces 'what happens' with the stickier 'how did we get here'.
examples: By the time I read it, the account was already gone.
He'd been lying for two years before any of us noticed.
The business was bankrupt weeks before I admitted it to myself.
She was already packing when I walked in.
The deal collapsed before the meeting even started.

applied_examples.hook: My savings were gone three months before I checked the statement.

name: The Confession
trigger: admitting fault disarms skepticism, signals honesty
template: I've been [doing X wrong / hiding Y] for [time], and here's the truth.
why_it_works: Vulnerability disarms the viewer before they can scroll because it reads as authentic, not salesy.
examples: I've been doing this wrong for two years.
I've never told anyone this about my business.
I lied about how I actually grew this account.
I almost quit last month and didn't tell a soul.
Confession: I faked confidence the entire time.

applied_examples.hook: I've been buying followers for a year and it ruined my reach. Here's the truth.

name: Costly Mistake Warning
template: I wasted [time/money] on [mistake] so you don't have to.
why_it_works: Framing your loss as the viewer's shortcut makes the warning feel like a gift, not a pitch.
examples: I wasted $30k on courses that taught me nothing.
I lost 2 years to this mistake so you don't have to.
I burned $5k testing these so you can skip them.
I made every mistake possible so your first time goes smoother.
I spent 6 months on the wrong strategy. Learn from me.

applied_examples.hook: I blew $10k on ads that didn't work so you can avoid these 3 traps.

name: Permission Reframe
trigger: relief from guilt, validation of forbidden feeling
template: It's okay to [guilt-laden action]. You're not [negative label], you're [positive reframe].
why_it_works: It releases a tension the viewer already carries, making them feel seen and grateful.
examples: It's okay to quit the job everyone says you're lucky to have.
It's okay to outgrow your old friends.
It's okay to rest. You're not lazy, you're depleted.
It's okay to want more. That's not greed, it's growth.
It's okay to go no-contact. You're not cruel, you're protecting yourself.

applied_examples.hook: It's okay to skip the gym today. You're not weak, you're recovering.

name: Called Out By Symptom
trigger: specific recognition triggers 'this is about me'
template: If you [exact specific symptom they're experiencing], this is for you.
why_it_works: Naming the precise symptom instead of the category makes the right person feel personally addressed.
examples: If your skin still breaks out no matter what you try, watch this.
If you reread the same paragraph five times, this is for you.
If your ROAS is slipping and you don't know why, stop scrolling.
If you lie awake replaying conversations, this one's for you.
If you've quit three diets this year, listen.

applied_examples.hook: If you manage paid social and your CPMs doubled overnight, this is for you.

name: Am I The Only One
trigger: social validation, hunt for shared experience
template: Am I the only one who [secretly relatable behavior]?
why_it_works: It invites the viewer to confirm a private habit is normal, which is irresistibly engaging.
examples: Am I the only one who rehearses arguments in the shower?
Am I the only one who opens the fridge with no plan?
Am I the only one who pretends to look busy when the boss walks by?
Am I the only one who has 47 browser tabs open right now?
Am I the only one who replies 'lol' while completely stone-faced?

applied_examples.hook: Am I the only one who makes a to-do list just to feel productive?

name: POV Relatable Scene
template: POV: you're [relatable situation] and [the awkward/honest truth].
why_it_works: Casting the viewer as the protagonist makes the moment feel like their own lived experience.
examples: POV: you're the only adult in the room and have no idea what you're doing.
POV: it's 2am and you're googling your own symptoms.
POV: you said 'you too' when the waiter said enjoy your meal.
POV: you're a working mom and this is the real morning routine.
POV: you finally have a free weekend and forgot how to relax.

applied_examples.hook: POV: payday hit and it's already gone by Tuesday.

name: Tell Me Without Telling Me
template: Tell me you're [identity/type] without telling me you're [that type].
why_it_works: It rewards insiders with instant recognition and dares them to see themselves in the answer.
examples: Tell me you're an oldest sibling without telling me.
Tell me you work in tech without telling me you work in tech.
Tell me you grew up in the 90s without telling me.
Tell me you're burnt out without saying you're burnt out.
Tell me you're new here without telling me you're new here.

applied_examples.hook: Tell me you skipped leg day without telling me you skipped leg day.

name: Curiosity Gap Tease
template: There's one [thing] about [topic] nobody tells you, and it changes everything.
why_it_works: An open information loop creates discomfort the brain resolves only by watching to the end.
examples: There's one step everyone skips, and it's why it never works.
Nobody tells you the real reason this fails.
There's a detail in this photo that changes the whole story.
One thing separates the people who make it. Watch.
The part nobody talks about is the part that matters.

applied_examples.hook: There's one thing chefs do before they cook that you never see.

name: Forbidden Knowledge
template: There's a reason no one talks about [topic]. I'm breaking that silence.
why_it_works: Framing information as suppressed makes it feel rare and valuable, raising perceived stakes.
examples: There's a reason no one talks about what happens after you go viral.
There's a reason your doctor won't bring this up.
There's a reason this trick isn't taught anywhere.
There's a reason the industry keeps this quiet. Here it is.
There's a reason no one in my family mentions 2009.

applied_examples.hook: There's a reason agents won't tell you the best month to buy. I will.

name: Unsettling Discovery
template: I found something in [ordinary place] that I can't explain.
why_it_works: An anomaly in a familiar setting triggers our threat-detection wiring and demands explanation.
examples: I found something in my grandmother's attic I can't explain.
I found a file on my work laptop that wasn't supposed to exist.
I found something in the old house that the inspector missed.
I opened the box and immediately knew something was wrong.
I found a message in my drafts I never wrote.

applied_examples.hook: I found an app on my phone draining data that I never installed.

name: Ignored Warning
template: Everyone told me not to [action]. I should have listened.
why_it_works: It promises a payoff of consequence, and the viewer stays to learn what went wrong.
examples: Everyone told me not to take the offer. I should have listened.
Everyone warned me about him. I should have listened.
They said don't refinance now. I didn't listen.
Everyone said don't quit without a plan. Here's what happened.
They told me to read the fine print. I should have listened.

applied_examples.hook: Everyone told me not to take the investor money. I should have listened.

name: Routine Disruption
trigger: stable pattern broken, surprise plus tension
template: I've [done normal routine] for [timeframe] until [the day everything changed].
why_it_works: Establishing a calm pattern then shattering it creates a sharp, magnetic narrative break.
examples: I've walked the same route for 5 years until last Tuesday.
I've ignored that email folder for months until I opened it.
I'd run the same numbers daily until the morning they didn't add up.
I packed the same lunch for a decade until the day I stopped.
I'd never missed a deadline in 10 years until this one.

applied_examples.hook: I'd loved my job for 8 years until the meeting that changed everything.

name: Contradiction Plus Promise
template: [Counterintuitive claim], and I'll prove it in [timeframe].
why_it_works: Contradicting common wisdom then promising proof forces the viewer to stay and judge.
examples: Longer videos actually get more views, and I'll prove it in 30 seconds.
Skipping breakfast made me more focused, here's the data.
Cold outreach still works in 2026, watch.
Working less made me earn more, I'll show you how.
Cheaper ads got me better results, here's why.

applied_examples.hook: Lifting lighter built more muscle for me, and I'll prove it.

name: You're Doing It Wrong
trigger: ego threat plus curiosity to self-correct
template: You've been [common activity] wrong your whole life. Here's the right way.
why_it_works: A mild ego threat about a daily habit creates urgency to fix something invisible.
examples: You've been reheating pizza wrong your whole life.
You've been charging your phone wrong this entire time.
You've been negotiating salary wrong, here's the fix.
You've been brushing your teeth wrong, dentists confirm.
You've been writing emails wrong and losing replies.

applied_examples.hook: You've been seasoning your steak wrong. Chefs do this instead.

name: Expert Contradiction
template: Every expert says [common belief], except the one who discovered [contradiction].
why_it_works: Pitting a lone authority against the consensus creates a high-stakes credibility showdown.
examples: Every nutritionist says breakfast matters, except the Harvard study that says otherwise.
Every coach says hustle, except the one who tracked 1,000 founders.
Every dermatologist pushes this, except the one who tested it.
Every guru says niche down, except the data that disagrees.
Every dentist recommends it, except the researcher who studied it.

applied_examples.hook: Every advisor says max your 401k, except the one who ran the math.

name: Unpopular Opinion
template: Unpopular opinion: [controversial take], and I'm tired of pretending otherwise.
why_it_works: Polarizing takes force the viewer to take a side, and either side keeps watching.
examples: Unpopular opinion: work-life balance is a myth.
Unpopular opinion: most productivity advice makes you slower.
Unpopular opinion: networking events are a waste of time.
Unpopular opinion: passion is overrated, skill pays.
Unpopular opinion: you don't need a morning routine.

applied_examples.hook: Unpopular opinion: going viral is the worst thing for your brand.

name: The Truth About
trigger: promise of hidden reality behind a facade
template: The truth about [topic everyone assumes they understand].
why_it_works: Implying everyone else has the wrong picture promises an insider correction worth staying for.
examples: The truth about what actually grows a following.
The truth about passive income nobody admits.
The truth about what therapists really think.
The truth about why your offer isn't selling.
The truth about overnight success.

applied_examples.hook: The truth about buying your first home that no agent will say out loud.

name: Nobody Talks About This
trigger: rarity signal plus fear of missing hidden info
template: Nobody talks about [overlooked part of topic], until it [matters].
why_it_works: Claiming an angle is untouched makes the content feel rare and the viewer feel ahead of the curve.
examples: Nobody talks about the messy middle of building a business.
Nobody talks about how lonely growth gets.
Nobody talks about what happens after the launch.
Nobody talks about the part where it stops being fun.
Nobody talks about this side of recovery.

applied_examples.hook: Nobody talks about the burnout that comes right after you blow up.

name: Biggest Mistake
trigger: loss aversion, fear of repeating an error
template: Here's the biggest mistake most people make with [topic].
why_it_works: People will watch to avoid a loss far more reliably than to chase a gain.
examples: Here's the biggest mistake people make with their resume.
The biggest mistake new investors make, every time.
The number one mistake killing your reach.
The biggest mistake couples make in the first year.
The mistake that quietly tanks most diets.

applied_examples.hook: The biggest onboarding mistake that's quietly churning your users.

name: Red Flag Alert
template: [Number] red flags that [bad outcome]. If you see #[X], [consequence].
why_it_works: Red flags promise protection from a hidden danger, and the consequence drives them to the end.
examples: 5 red flags your 'friend' is actually toxic.
3 red flags your job is about to do layoffs.
4 red flags a deal is a scam.
Red flags your skincare is making it worse.
5 red flags in a rental listing.

applied_examples.hook: 4 red flags in a job interview that mean you should run.

name: Before You Buy
trigger: loss aversion at the decision moment
template: Before you buy [product/decision], watch this.
why_it_works: Intercepting someone right before a purchase decision makes the warning feel urgently relevant.
examples: Before you buy the viral cleaning gadget, watch this test.
Before you sign that lease, read this.
Before you book that flight, do this first.
Before you buy a course, watch this 20 seconds.
Before you upgrade your phone, see this.

applied_examples.hook: Before you buy the $89 version, watch me test the $8 dupe.

name: Social Proof Volume
template: [Big number] people [did this/got this result]. Here's how.
why_it_works: When unsure, people copy the crowd, so volume of others instantly raises credibility.
examples: 12,000 people used this exact script. Here's how.
Over 50,000 readers can't be wrong about this.
Half a million people tried this hack last month.
10,000 founders agree on this one thing.
Everyone in my group chat is doing this now.

applied_examples.hook: 40,000 people lost the last 10 pounds this way. Here's the method.

name: Everyone Is Doing It
trigger: FOMO plus belonging, fear of being left out
template: Everyone in [in-group] is already [doing X] and you're behind.
why_it_works: Belonging to a moving in-group is a strong pull, and being left behind stings.
examples: Everyone in marketing is quietly using this tool.
Every top creator switched to this format already.
Everyone in your industry is already doing this.
All the smart money moved here months ago.
Everyone your age figured this out but you.

applied_examples.hook: Everyone in finance already moved their cash here. You're late.

name: Borrowed Authority
template: A [credentialed expert] told me [surprising thing], and it changed everything.
why_it_works: People defer to credible experts, so borrowing authority front-loads trust into the claim.
examples: A Harvard sleep scientist told me to stop doing this.
A retired FBI negotiator taught me this one line.
A Michelin chef told me the salt rule is wrong.
A burnout therapist gave me this 10-second test.
A top VC told me what actually kills startups.

applied_examples.hook: A child psychologist told me to never say this phrase. Here's why.

name: Insider Credentials
template: As a [role] with [X years/credential], here's what I'd never do.
why_it_works: Stated experience grants permission to make bold claims and promises rare insider truth.
examples: As an ER nurse for 12 years, here's what I'd never do.
As a recruiter, here's the resume line I always skip.
As a mechanic, here's the car I'd never buy.
As a chef, here's the dish I never order out.
As a lawyer, here's the contract clause I always remove.

applied_examples.hook: As an agent for 15 years, here's the house feature I'd never pay for.

name: Investigation Opening
trigger: mystery plus payoff promise of disturbing find
template: I spent [timeframe] investigating [question]. What I found was [unsettling].
why_it_works: Effort plus an ominous teaser signals a payoff big enough to justify staying.
examples: I spent 4 months tracking down everyone in that viral photo.
I spent a year auditing 'passive income' gurus. It's worse than you think.
I spent 6 weeks inside the industry. What I saw shocked me.
I read every page of the terms. You won't believe what's in there.
I dug into where the money actually goes. It's disturbing.

applied_examples.hook: I spent 3 months reverse-engineering the algorithm. What I found surprised me.

name: Hidden History
template: In [year/place], something happened that changed everything, and no one knows.
why_it_works: An untold origin promises rare knowledge the viewer can feel smart for discovering.
examples: In 1952 a lab accident created something you use daily.
In 1987, a decision in one room reshaped the internet.
There's a forgotten story behind the thing in your pocket.
A mistake in 1971 is why your money works the way it does.
Nobody remembers the woman who invented this.

applied_examples.hook: In 1908 a chemist's accident created the flavor you crave most.

name: Hidden Connection
template: What do [unrelated thing A] and [thing B] have in common?
why_it_works: Two unrelated things create a puzzle the brain wants resolved before it can move on.
examples: What do Pringles and airplane wings have in common?
What does your coffee have to do with your anxiety?
What do casinos and your phone share? More than you think.
What links your posture and your confidence?
What do bees and the stock market have in common?

applied_examples.hook: What do your gut and your mood have in common? Almost everything.

name: Shocking Stat
trigger: surprise plus self-relevance of a number
template: [Shocking statistic] about [topic]. Here's why it matters to you.
why_it_works: A precise, surprising number feels credible and makes the viewer check if it applies to them.
examples: 84% of your downloaded apps track your location right now.
92% of resumes never get read by a human.
Most people only use 10% of what they pay for.
1 in 3 subscriptions you have, you forgot about.
73% of New Year goals die by February.

applied_examples.hook: 84% of apps are tracking you right now. Here's how to check yours.

name: Transformation Timeline
template: [Timeframe] ago I was [struggling]. Today I'm [success]. Here's what changed.
why_it_works: A concrete before-and-after gives instant proof the result is achievable and a reason to stay for the how.
examples: 6 months ago I was broke. Today I'm debt-free. Here's how.
A year ago I couldn't run a mile. Today I ran 10.
Two years ago I had 0 followers. Now I have 200k.
Last winter I was burnt out. Now I work half as much.
90 days ago I couldn't focus. Now I finish everything.

applied_examples.hook: 8 weeks ago I couldn't do one pushup. Today I did 50. Here's the plan.

name: One Change Did It
template: This one small change completely transformed my [area].
why_it_works: The promise of a single easy lever feels low-cost and high-reward, lowering the bar to watch.
examples: This one tweak doubled my reply rate.
One small change fixed my sleep overnight.
Changing this one habit transformed my mornings.
One sentence changed how I negotiate forever.
This single shift cut my expenses in half.

applied_examples.hook: This one calendar change gave me back 10 hours a week.

name: The Hero's Problem
trigger: StoryBrand: name the villain/problem viewer feels
template: If [the problem] keeps [tormenting you], it's not your fault. Here's why.
why_it_works: Naming the viewer's problem as the villain makes them the hero and you the guide, building instant trust.
examples: If you keep starting over every Monday, it's not your fault.
If your content flops despite effort, here's the real reason.
If you're always busy but never done, the system is broken, not you.
If you can't save no matter what, it's not discipline.
If your team keeps missing deadlines, it's not them.

applied_examples.hook: If you keep procrastinating on the thing that matters, it's not laziness. Here's why.

name: Once Upon A Time Spine
trigger: Pixar story spine, narrative pull of 'and then'
template: Every day [normal], until one day [disruption], and because of that [stakes].
why_it_works: The Pixar spine builds escalating causation that the brain follows almost involuntarily.
examples: Every day I ignored the numbers, until one day the bank called.
Every day she said yes, until the day she finally said no.
Every day I posted and got nothing, until I changed one thing.
Every day the team coasted, until the client walked.
Every day I played it safe, until safe became dangerous.

applied_examples.hook: Every day we chased features, until one day a user told us the truth.

name: Scarcity Window
template: This [opportunity] is disappearing, and most people will miss it.
why_it_works: We want things more when they're vanishing, and 'most will miss it' adds exclusivity.
examples: This loophole closes at the end of the month.
This is the last window before prices change.
Most people will miss this and regret it.
This trend has weeks left before it's saturated.
By next year this won't work anymore.

applied_examples.hook: This tax window closes in 6 weeks and most people won't use it.

name: You're Already Too Late
trigger: loss aversion plus urgency to catch up
template: If you haven't [done X] yet, you're already behind. Here's how to catch up.
why_it_works: The fear of having missed out is sharper than the desire to gain, sparking immediate attention.
examples: If you haven't done this yet, you're already behind.
If your business isn't doing this in 2026, you're losing.
If you're not using this yet, your competitors are.
If you haven't claimed this, you missed half of it.
If you're just hearing about this, you're late, but not too late.

applied_examples.hook: If your brand isn't on this platform yet, you're already behind. Catch up here.

name: Open Loop Cliffhanger
trigger: Zeigarnik effect, open loops nag the mind
template: Wait until you see [what happens at the end] — but first, [setup].
why_it_works: An unfinished promise stays nagging in the mind until the viewer sees the resolution.
examples: Wait until you see how this ends.
The ending is the part you need to see.
Stick around, because what happens last changes everything.
I'll show you the result, but watch how I get there.
Don't skip, the twist is at the end.

applied_examples.hook: This looked like a disaster, but wait until you see the final reveal.

name: Pattern Interrupt Shock
template: [Unexpected action/statement that defies what they expected].
why_it_works: Something that violates expectations snaps the brain out of autopilot and onto your video.
examples: Stop. Don't make this dinner tonight.
Throw away your to-do list. Seriously.
I'm about to ruin your favorite snack for you.
Delete this app right now, I'll explain.
Put down your coffee for this one.

applied_examples.hook: Cancel that subscription before you finish this video.

name: Direct Question
template: What would you do if [provocative scenario]?
why_it_works: A direct question hijacks attention because the brain can't help but start answering it.
examples: What would you do with one free day a week?
What if everything you know about saving is wrong?
Why does this keep happening to you?
What's the one thing you'd never admit out loud?
How much of your day do you actually control?

applied_examples.hook: What would you do if you got laid off tomorrow? Most people have no answer.

name: Bet You Didn't Know
template: I bet you didn't know this about [familiar thing].
why_it_works: A friendly challenge to the viewer's knowledge baits them to prove or disprove it.
examples: I bet you didn't know your phone does this.
Bet you didn't know why we yawn.
I bet you've never noticed this on a dollar bill.
Bet you didn't know this about your own name.
I guarantee you didn't know this hides in plain sight.

applied_examples.hook: I bet you didn't know airlines do this with your seat assignment.

name: Cheap vs Expensive
template: I tried the $[low] version and the $[high] version of [thing]. Here's the truth.
why_it_works: A head-to-head with a likely upset keeps the viewer for the verdict.
examples: I tried the $8 dupe and the $89 original. I'm keeping the dupe.
$3 coffee vs $300 machine. The winner shocked me.
Cheap flight vs premium. Was it worth it?
$15 knife vs $200 knife. Honest test.
Free tool vs paid tool. The free one won.

applied_examples.hook: I tested the $12 serum against the $120 one. The result changed my routine.

name: Numbered Insider List
template: [Number] things [insiders] know about [topic] but won't tell you. #[X] is wild.
why_it_works: A specific count promises a contained payoff and the teased item drives them to the end.
examples: 3 things your mechanic won't tell you. #2 saves $3k.
5 things recruiters notice in 6 seconds.
4 things landlords hope you never learn.
3 things doctors do for their own colds.
5 things every editor cuts but never explains.

applied_examples.hook: 3 things trainers do that they'd never sell you. Number 2 is free.

name: Tier List Provocation
template: Ranking [category] worst to best. #1 will surprise you / my F-tier will start a fight.
why_it_works: Rankings invite disagreement, and a teased controversial pick guarantees watch-through.
examples: Ranking fast food breakfasts worst to best. #1 shocked me.
Putting productivity apps in tier list. F-tier will anger people.
Ranking every diet I tried. The winner was free.
Tier-listing morning habits. Most are useless.
Ranking side hustles by real ROI. Top one surprised me.

applied_examples.hook: Ranking every note app worst to best. My F-tier pick will make people mad.

name: The Mechanism
trigger: promise of a repeatable system, not luck
template: Here's the exact [system/method] I used to [achieve result].
why_it_works: Promising an exact, repeatable method makes the result feel attainable and worth the watch.
examples: Here's the exact script I used to land the client.
Here's the 3-step system that fixed my sleep.
Here's exactly how I cut my expenses in half.
Here's the framework I use to write every hook.
Here's the routine that got me out of debt.

applied_examples.hook: Here's the exact 4-line email that books me 10 meetings a week.

name: The Simple Trick
template: This [number]-second trick [solves annoying problem] instantly.
why_it_works: A tiny effort for an instant fix is an irresistible value proposition.
examples: This 10-second trick reheats pizza perfectly.
This 5-second hack fixes a stuck zipper.
This one trick stops your phone from dying so fast.
This 15-second method falls asleep faster.
This quick fix stops the squeaky door instantly.

applied_examples.hook: This 30-second trick removes any stain you thought was permanent.

name: The Bold Promise
template: Do this and [specific result] in [timeframe], guaranteed.
why_it_works: A specific, time-bound promise sets a concrete payoff that's hard to scroll past.
examples: Do this and double your replies in a week.
Follow this and sleep through the night in 3 days.
Try this and never run out of content ideas.
Use this and your emails get opened, period.
Do this once and never lose your keys again.

applied_examples.hook: Do this for 10 days and your posture fixes itself. I'll prove it.

name: Awe Reveal
trigger: awe, scale or beauty arrests attention
template: You're looking at [astonishing thing], and what it does is unbelievable.
why_it_works: Awe captures attention and triggers sharing, holding the viewer in wonder.
examples: You're looking at the largest living thing on Earth.
This tiny chip holds more than every library combined.
What this machine does in one second took humans a year.
You're seeing a sound made visible.
This is what one drop of ocean water actually contains.

applied_examples.hook: You're looking at a photo of a single atom. What it reveals is staggering.

name: Nostalgia Trigger
template: Remember when [shared past experience]? Here's what we got wrong.
why_it_works: Nostalgia creates a warm, identity-linked bond and pulls the viewer into a shared memory.
examples: Remember when we burned CDs for road trips?
Remember waiting all week for one episode?
Remember when the whole class went silent for the overhead projector?
Remember saving up for one game?
Remember when phones just made calls?

applied_examples.hook: Remember dial-up internet? Here's what that wait actually taught us.

name: Status Aspiration
template: The [small habit] that separates [aspirational group] from everyone else.
why_it_works: Tying a behavior to a status group lets viewers aspire to belong by adopting it.
examples: The morning habit that separates millionaires from the rest.
What confident people do that you don't.
The one thing top 1% earners never do.
What people who never get sick all have in common.
The habit that separates leaders from followers.

applied_examples.hook: The one email habit that separates high performers from everyone else.

name: Anger Injustice Opener
template: It's not fair that [injustice], and nobody's saying it.
why_it_works: Anger at injustice is a potent driver that compels people to engage and share.
examples: It's not fair that they raised prices and shrank the product.
Nobody's saying how rigged this actually is.
It's wrong that you pay more for doing nothing different.
They profit while you struggle, and it's by design.
It makes me angry that no one warned you about this.

applied_examples.hook: It's not fair that banks earn billions on your savings and pay you pennies.

name: Two-Word Provocation
template: [One or two jarring words]. [Then the turn.]
why_it_works: Radical brevity feels like a record scratch, breaking the scroll rhythm and demanding the rest.
examples: Stop dieting.
You're overpaying.
It's a scam.
Quit now.
Read this twice.

applied_examples.hook: Delete it. Your best post is the one you're afraid to publish.

name: Origin Of Ordinary
trigger: novelty, hidden backstory of familiar thing
template: The reason [everyday thing] is the way it is will blow your mind.
why_it_works: Promising a surprising origin for something mundane reframes the familiar as fascinating.
examples: The reason QWERTY keyboards exist will blow your mind.
The reason elevators have mirrors is genius.
Why your jeans have a tiny pocket, finally explained.
The reason gas pumps click off is wild.
Why airplane windows are round will surprise you.

applied_examples.hook: The reason restaurants put bread on the table first is pure psychology.

name: Honest Test Verdict
template: I bought [hyped product] so you don't have to. Here's the honest verdict.
why_it_works: A firsthand, no-stakes test reads as unbiased proof, earning trust before the verdict lands.
examples: I bought the viral leggings so you don't have to. Shocked.
I tested the $200 gadget everyone's obsessed with. Verdict inside.
I tried the trend for 30 days. Here's the honest result.
I bought all 5 so you only need one.
I used it for a month. Save your money or not?

applied_examples.hook: I tried the sold-out viral serum for 2 weeks so you don't have to. Honest verdict.
